message:
fix: add mutual exclusivity check and fix yq query syntax

1. dsr: Add check that --no-sync and --sync-only cannot be used together
   These flags are semantically opposite - one skips sync entirely while
   the other exits after sync. Using both is always a user error.

2. act_runner.sh: Fix yq v4 query syntax for matrix entries
   The complex if/then expression wasn't compatible with yq v4.
   Simplified to use select() with direct value access.

Found during fresh-eyes code review.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1713,6 +1713,12 @@ EOF
 
     log_info "Targets: $target_list"
 
+    # Check for mutually exclusive sync flags
+    if $no_sync && $sync_only; then
+        log_error "--no-sync and --sync-only are mutually exclusive"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
     # Sync source to remote hosts (unless --no-sync)
     if ! $no_sync; then
         log_info "Syncing source to remote build hosts..."

@@ -515,10 +515,8 @@ act_get_flags() {
         .act_matrix."'"$platform"'" // {} |
         to_entries |
         .[] |
-        .key as $k |
-        (if (.value | type) == "array" then .value[] else .value end) |
-        select(. != null and . != "") |
-        "\($k):\(. | tostring)"
+        select(.value != null and .value != "") |
+        .key + ":" + (.value | tostring)
     ' "$config_file" 2>/dev/null)
     if [[ -n "$matrix_entries" ]]; then
         while IFS= read -r entry; do
